name: FootbagDenmark
description:
FootbagDenmark is the central footbag organization in Denmark, 
initiated by experienced players. The purpose of the organization is to 
make footbag more recognised and more popular in Denmark. We 
encourage and support everybody, to start their own regional or local 
footbag club. FD hosts the national championships and represents Denmark 
in EFC with two members. 
The club is located in Copenhagen where most activities take place. We are 
approx. 35 members and more to come. The style is new as well as old 
school, and you can find everything from beginners to champions among FD?s 
members.

name: United Flyers of Sonoma, UFOS
description:
The United Flyers of Sonoma, UFOS, have been established as the top 
alternative sports club in Sonoma County, California since the early 
1980's.  Supporting both Disc and Footbag Sports, the UFOS have 
hosted many major events like the annual Indian Summer Festival and is
the birth place of the Western Regional Footbag Championships.  

Historical UFOS members include Disc Freestyle Master and World Champion, 
Kevin "Skippy" Givens and Footbag Hall of Famer, Chris Ott. 

The UFOS need a new start with fresh members.  Join this club and continue the tradition for the UFOS.  We can find a place to practice and get something started.  Who is in?

name: Nashville Kicks
description:
We started back in '02 at Middle Tennessee State University 
(MTSU) when everyone was shredding. In 2005 the freestyle 
scene died, so we switched gears & started playing net. In 
2010 the net scene was also on its last leg. What did we 
do? We found the nearest buddhist temple & picked up a takraw 
ball. Nashville has sizeable Laotian & Burmese communities 
that almost play takraw on a daily basis here. You can check 
out our facebook page at Nashville Kicks.

name: FOOTBAG ERIE
description:
FOOTBAG ERIE is just a bunch of people who love to kick. The idea of starting 
FOOTBAG ERIE was to unify kickers from the greater Erie Pennsylvania area, 
to spread the word about the sport and to become better kickers. From the thaw in spring to the chill of fall, the members of FOOTBAG ERIE go,
go, go. Our members travel all over the north eastern states like New Jersey,
and Maryland to visit other footbag-ers and to attend tournaments. Some,
including the one and the only, footbag legend, JACK LENTZ (aka Hacky Sack
Jack), even travel as far as Europe for the Footbag Worlds Tournament. We are fans of a full spectrum of foot sports. We actively play and compete in
Freestyle, Net, Golf, and even Four Square. We practice at a number of places
around town. There is no schedule, we get together when the weather is good
and when we have the time to show up. We try to get together AT LEAST once a week. Oh but that's not all, we also offer demonstrations. Whether for the general public or special groups, the FOOTBAG ERIE crew has has traveled all over to give informative demonstration. We have worked with area high schools, private groups, and even Job Corps located in New York State. Our presentations offer a wealth of information including: The history of footbag Explanations of the various footbag sports and tournaments, Explanations of the health benefits of footbag (Often, our demos are used to encourage children to be more physically active and to offer an alternative form of physical therapy to those who need it), And of course, the demos! Not only will we show what we've got but we work with the group, and allow them to get involved and have a little hands on fun. We work for hourly rates or per event. If you are interested contact JACK LENTZ for booking.

name: Knoxville Footbag Association
description:
What up shredaholics,

This is the main domain for the Knoxville Footbag Association (KFA). We are many members strong and are trying to get the K-town footbag scene in full motion. This is the only footbag club in our area and we welcome ALL skill levels.
Novices to master-shredders will have a good time in our circles. We have some folks with 5 ADD tricks and we have a few newcomers starting with toe stalls. Whether you want to come learn new tricks or help spread the skills to others, get your ass down here to kick with us.
You can check out some of our videos on Youtube and on our Facebook page.

We try to reach as many folks as we can and would love to have any potential members show up - or if you are just passing through town, we could host a circle. Either way come down and support your local footbag club.

name: Boston Shred Crew
description:
Greets-

you have stumbled upon the official footbag.org club listing for the Boston
Shred Crew(BSC).  There really isnt any other freestyle club in Boston, and
we're probably the biggest/most active in MA.  So dont bother contacting any
of the other listed clubs.  We kick at least once a week, on saturdays at 1:00
at Northeastern University near Fenway.  We have indoor locations to kick,
outdoor locations to kick, several dv cameras for taking footage, a pimpin
boombox, plenty of bags to kick on, and about 4-7 regular members that show up
at least once a week.  we also play randomly during the week.  If you live in
or are visiting Boston in the near future, and want to get better at keeping
the bag off the ground, or are just looking for someone to kick with, or if
you like run-on sentences, definitely feel free to contact me via email or my
listed cell phone number.

одежду, обувь и др. эквип); - организуем тренировки, показательные выступления, совернования; - популяризируем футбэг в СМИ. Наше коммьюнити открыто для всех. Присоединятесь! =) Welcome Footbag Empire =)! Our main goal is making footbag the most popular sport. We are doing everything for tou to enjoy footbag: - produce and distribute everything for our sport (footbags, clothes, shoes
and all the other stuff); - organize trainings, shows and competitions; - promote footbag on mass media; Our community is open for everyone. Join us! =)

Hey everyone, My name is Nick Polini.  I'm an alumni of Penn State University with a Wildlife Biology degree (Biology minor) and I'm currently living in Drumore, PA (Lancaster County), working as a Biologist.

My club, PAAAFA gets together every other weekend, around Myerstown or Lancaster City. We're accepting of any level player. We teach basics to anyone eager to learn, we shred and challenge each other, and while we take practicing footbag seriously, we also like to have a lot of fun. The club consists of about 8 active players from Drumore, Birdsboro, Myerstown, and Hershey area. 

Contact me if interested. Cell phone is your best bet. 

Cell- 484-995-5639
Email- Nickpolini@gmail.com
Facebook-www.facebook.com/nick.polini.3

name: High Altitude Hacky Hangtime & Punning Society
description:
We play what can be described as "go for it" hack. If it is within the realm of human possibility (or slightly beyond)to kick it, it must be pursued. We enjoy kicks which go high and involve a lot of hang time. Scoops are a favorite trick- by 'scoop' I mean picking it up on the bounce and keeping it in play. We play with hard footbags like the Juice or the less firmly packed Kanga.

That was Zen, this is now.

There are 4 regular participants and 2 or 3 irregulars. New people are welcome. 

Call (307)761-2129 for more info.

name: TSB - The Shred Brothers
description:
This is our "old school" freestyle club 
Please old members join it now so we can have some history here :)

The club was started by Pavel, Artur, Ivo and Lasko in 2001 (Sofia) and quickly expanded to other cities as our friends joined us.

We were and are the main freestyle footbag club in Bulgaria.

Our team members have won all of the Bulgarian local freestyle competitions from 2002 - 2008
We had some Todexon participations

In net footbag we have reached finals 3 times on doubles (won one of them in Varna)

The club was deactivated in 2008, and was restarted in 2014

Most recent club reunion happened on 17th European footbag championship in Sofia Bulgaria 2015
You can follow us on https://www.facebook.com/TheShredBrothers

name: Boise Foot Clan
description:
We are happy to introduce the Boise Foot Clan, the new/restructured footbag club in Boise Idaho! We are dedicated to the sport footbag, disciplines within this include freestyle (doing tricks and combinations of tricks), casual kicking, footbag net, and footbag four square. The group caters to all skill levels from beginner/casual circle kicker to professional level freestyle. Throughout the week we have many meet ups, if you are looking to connect and kick please see the contact info below. Our group is friendly and welcoming so please come on down and check us out! 

Here's some of our freestyle footbag players. Among them are multiple members of the Big Add Posse, an elite group of players dedicated to pushing the limits of the game. Included in the club are;

Jim Penske; 5 time US Champion, 2nd at World Footbag Championships (Routines) 2007.
Nick Landes; US Circle Champion, 2nd at World Footbag Championships (Routines) 2015.
Daryl Genz; 4 time Doubles Worlds Champion.
Sunny Freeman-Genz; US Champion.
Red Husted; Baller.
New member soon to arrive: Evan Gatesman (World Circle Champion 2014, World Shred 30 Champion 2015, World Request Champion 2015). See you in the circles!

name: Tucson Wild Footbag Cats
description:
Hello freestyle footbag players! 

My name is Aaron, and I have been involved with the sport of footbag since 2002, when I went to my first Footbag World Championships in San Francisco. I won the intermediate freestyle Canadian Championships one year later in Vancouver, B.C., and since then have tried to stay active with the sport for fun, health, and because I simply love this game and trying to hit new tricks. 

I also learned how to stitch footbags in 2005, and if you would like a very good high quality footbag, just let me know and I will stitch one up for you. 

I play footbag here in Tucson by myself usually about 2-3 times per week. I am interested in reaching out to people in Tucson that have interest in learning more about the sport, and to play with anyone and everyone that likes to play footbag traveling through the area.

name: Footbag Player Association of India
description:
Footbag Players Association India (FPA India)

I am the President of Footbag Players Association India (FPA India), an organization proudly registered under the MSME Registration Act, Government of India.

Our Vision

To discover, develop, and nurture talented athletes in the sport of Footbag, transforming them into world-class players who not only represent India on international platforms but also bring glory to the nation by achieving top positions.

Our Commitment

FPA India is firmly committed to promoting Footbag across the country by:

Organizing training camps, workshops, and tournaments to build competitive skills.

Providing players with modern techniques, advanced training methods, and international exposure.

Creating a professional pathway for athletes to excel both nationally and globally.

Developing a culture of discipline, teamwork, and sportsmanship through structured programs.

Our Goal

As we prepare our athletes for upcoming international events and championships, we aim to ensure they receive:
> Latest coaching techniques aligned with global standards
> Access to professional training infrastructure
> Guidance from experienced coaches and mentors
> Opportunities to compete internationally

Through these efforts, FPA India strives to not only raise champions but also build a strong future for Footbag in India, inspiring the next generation of players.

name: New Haven Elimination Footbag
description:
Welcome to New Haven Elimination Footbag! If you've ever seen or played standard hacky sack/footbag, then we encourage you to check out the latest competitive iteration of the sport for a whole new perspective on footbag itself. Yes, we kind of throw the whole notion of 'Peace through Play' into a bit of a conundrum, however, come see how we do things and you'll quickly be swayed on how physically demanding, mentally challenging, and utterly exhilarating Elimination Footbag can be!

Have questions about Elimination Footbag or the club? Please contact silvio@footbag.org for more information. 

*PLEASE NOTE: The club is just getting it's feet off the ground, with a goal of setting up a regular kicking schedule in the coming weeks, so please be patient and feel free to contact me (Silvio) for any further information or to express interest in joining when we start up.*

Hackrifice was founded by Orien Louttit, Jeremie Yuen, and Joel Dion in 
1998. There's a story but I can't tell it well enough; ask Joel. we host an annual tournament, locally, in late September. The tournament 
accommodates both freestyle and net. Events include 30-second shred, 
sick three, big trick, and singles and doubles net. If you live in the area or are passing through, stop on by and shred 
with us. Remember, there are only two kinds of moves: those you can hit, and those you can't hit yet !

name: BC Soramäki Indians
description:
We play net and freestyle. Everyone who is interested in footbag is
welcome to play with us.
Eli siis tervetuloa kaikki pelaamaan meidän rinkiin. Kaikki saavat tulla.

name: FC Footstar Berlin e.V.
description:
The FC Footstar Berlin e.V. is Germany's oldest and biggest footbag club. Founded in 1998, we are still active and training regularly.
There are two scheduled indoor training sessions on weekends. During summertime, there is casual outdoor shred & net sessions throughout the week as well. Just get in contact with one of us to find out where or when.

name: Sole Rebels Zürich
description:
Unser Club ist leider nicht mehr aktiv. Einmal pro Jahr treffen wir uns noch.
Melde dich doch bei Fragen :)

name: 1. Rien N'est Hacky - RNH Footbag
description:
Greatings footbaggers, if you plan to visit Paris, please get in touch with us. It doesn't matter 
if you are circle kicker, net player or freestyler. We are always keen to 
meet players from around the world.

name: OOPS! Freestyle
description:
We are OOPS! Freestyle Membership in OOPS! Location is 
irrelevant... only style matters. Members: OOPS! Local: Mike Stoler Greg Grandy Sam Gregory Chad Devlahovich Eric Windsor Aaron Omatsu, Jesse Shriber + Delvon Itay Kahaner Bryan Fournier Christian in Riverside(Go Austria!) Chris Pinkus Jeremy Mirken Nate Maertens (Dr. Ridiculous) Malcolm Gultt (MovieStar in Action) Tom Wheeler (M.I.A.) Sean Fura Satya Leonard Dat Phan Marc Phillips OOPS! Extraterrestrial: Mike Jauss (New Mexico) Josh Childs (N.Y) James Risden ("Riz", C.I.C.) Jim Penske (Idaho) Spencer Behm (Utah)

name: Tallinn Footbag Club
description:
Tallinn Footbag Club was established on December 20th, 1999 and has been the 
driving force for footbag in Estonia ever since. The scene here is mostly 
focused on freestyle, but occasinal net sessions take place too. We welcome 
any- and everybody who wants to kick with us to one of our trainings (see 
below). If you are passing by Tallinn or any other place in Estonia, be sure 
to get in touch with us first :)

Tallinna Footbagi Klubi asutati 20. detsembril 1999 ja on sellest ajast 
alates olnud Eesti footbagi elu juhtiv organisatsioon. Siinne stseen on 
peamiselt vabastiili-keskne, kuid vahest korraldatakse ka mõned neti 
sessioonid. Ootame kõiki oma treeningutele, sõltumata oskustest, vanusest ja 
soost (lisainfo leiad alt)

name: SG Frankfurt Footbag e.V.
description: SG Frankfurt Footbag e.V. An den Röthen 29 60389 Frankfurt/Main Germany Mail: info@frankfurtfootbag.de

name: Santa Cruz Footbag Club
description:
Santa Cruz Footbag Club meets weekly in Santa Cruz.  
New and experienced players are always welcome.

Santa Cruz is a culturally progressive community. We encourage players 
of all kinds to come and join us for 
kicking, socializing and to experience 
the famous Santa Cruz lifestyle --  8^)

It's always good to call and let us know you're coming so we can be sure and
meet you.  E-mail Chris Young (Catfish@footbag.org)for exact information.

name: Chicago Inner Circle
description:
2008 OPEN INVITATION:
Despite how often this page and our personal profiles are updated, we are
active and playing all year round. It's 2008 and we would like to extend
another open invitation to kickers of all levels and disciplines to come out
and play with us. Although primarily a freestyle club, we've had sessions last
year where freestyle, net & 4-square is played at the same time. Get a hold of
one or our contacts or you can also check out any recent updates in the club's
blog on the Modified Forums. ABOUT CIC:
We have been making a name for ourselves and spreading our own brand of
footbag fun around since May of 2000. Over the years we've inducted a few
members and lost a few but the core group is still in tact and we intend to be
a presence in the footbag community for quite a while. To try and help promote
and spread the sport of footbag, we've done several demos and ran local
tournaments. We've also helped to make the annual New Years Jam at McCormick
Place a huge success drawing players from as far as California, New York and
Canada. But when it's all said and done, we are just a group of close friends
that like to spend our free time kicking a footbag, hanging out and having a
good time. On 5-19-08 we will officially have been around for 8 years but will be
planning to celebrate it later in the summer with a proper CIC B-day Jam.
Happy 8th B-day Chicago Inner Circle!!!

name: Houston Skyliners Footbag Club
description: Welcome to the "Houston Skyliners", founded in 1980.  We play Net, Freestyle, Golf, and Consecutive footbag. We also compete in tournaments.  Feel free to join us..  All that can happen is a great time and maybe a few new moves. Click for video. Contact Tim, Heather or myself for further information. Garry

name: Christchurch Footbag Club
description: If you live in Christchurch, you have an opportunity to become a great freestyler. We have a net, too. It's always good fun. So, it would be awesome if you get in contact with one of us to set up a time/place to kick!

name: (210) COBRA crew
description:
ATTENTION VAGRANTS OF THE SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA AREA.....the 
notoriuos 210 crew want you to come kick....for more info email me, tom 
wheeler at iastroproject@hotmail.com, or Nate-Dogg at 
nasty_nate@footbag.org.  Other members include Malcolm G, Kaz, Big 
Devin, and the GCC crew....kicking til the early 
morning.......join or die--if it aint 210, it aint crap

name: Canadian Footbag Alliance
description:
Please contact the Canadian Footbag Alliance for info on Footbag in
Canada.  

www.FootbagCanada.com.

Building an Alliance for the Sport of Footbag.

Kick on!

name: PSF Polish Footbag Association
description:
Welcome everyone who wants to join us!
We are open to kick with people that are interested in footbag or wanna get 
some information about this sport.
We are the first club in Poland since footbag came up in our country.
for more informations you can check our website: www.footbag.com.pl
if you are from different country and want to visit Poland please let us 
know before you come.
maybe we can help you to get place to stay.

name: Raleigh Freestyle Footbag Club
description:
News as of 4/25/04:

My club at school will most likely split up at the end of this year when I 
go to East Carolina University for college.  I am hoping that we will have 
enough interest there to get the pace back up by second semester of my 
freshman year.  If you live in Greenville, NC... please contact me with 
information so we can get together.

name: Ministry of Silly Walks
description:
We're a club dedicated to the game, and we welcome 
all players.  We enjoy a casual kicking session, but mostly we skool serious 
freestyle.  To that end, some of our members have established a school of 
footbag to offer beginner and advanced classes to help your game progress as 
quickly as possible.  Call or e-mail us for more details on classes or 
regular club sessions.

name: Footbag-Vienna
description:
SHOUT-OUTS AN ALLE FOOTBAGER !!! Man nehme einen Kreis, stecke 2 - 10 Leute 
      hinein, und werfe ihnen einen Footbag zu !!! WAS PASSIERT IST 
ERSTAUNLICH Sie spielen mit dem Bag, lachen, plaudern, und amusieren sich, bis 
entweder 
        die Sonne untergeht oder die letzte U-Bahn fährt. www.footbag.at www.footbag-vienna.com

name: UNITY FOOTBAG CLUB
description:
Attention fellow footbaggers and Hacky Sackers,
Unity invites all levels of players to enjoy what Arizona has to offer. Our 
club is primarily focused on Freestyle, but we keep a Jimmy C (NET) on hand 
at all times. The kicking schedule remains constant and will be updated as 
changes occur. If you are in the general area of Tucson/Phoenix and would 
like to play or have questions, please pick up the phone. You will find our 
dedication to the sport evident when you call or send e-mail.

Take Care,
Johnny & Mike

name: The Rochester Footbag Association
description:
Thanks for checking us out.  We are a strictly freestyle footbag club 
based in Rochester, NY, and comprised of U of 
R students and Rochester-area footbag enthusiasts.  We welcome players of 
all skill levels, including hack players (we have a few converts in our 
group ;) ).  If you're in the Rochester area, please drop by.  We have a 
very active club with MANY impromptu shred sessions, and some really 
promising freestyle.  

Hope to see you out there.
    
http://www.rochesterfootbag.com - club home page

name: Icarus Footbag
description:
Welcome to everybody who want to play with us.
We have the luck to live in the south of france so we practice freestyle 
everywhere. It will be a pleasure to welcome anybody from France or anywhere to 
play with us. For more informations : www.footbag.fr/forum e-mail us to meet each other

name: Footcraft
description:
Footcraft was born in 2003. We operate in Turku and around 
south-western Finland. We play net and net only. Don't 
hesitate to contact us if you are around and want to have your ass handed to you on the court. Off-court we're nice, I promise!

name: FootFighter
description:
Hi! We are team fo young, energic pople who are loved in footbag.
We are playing for joy and pleasure. We most like freestyle but we also 
do everything what is connected with footbaging. 

We also make best freestyle footbags in the world. FootFighter 
freestyle footbags :))

name: AIR-A-ZONA FOOTBAG NET CLUB
description:
Rob Adams new phone number is 

805-440-1591.  

He is in town during the winter months.  Give him a call to play net.  No regular kicks scheduled.

name: Association Québécoise de Footbag
description:
Bienvenue! L'AQF se concentre surtout sur le Footbag au filet mais peut relayer de 
l'information sur toute autre discipline du Footbag au besoin

name: The Auckland Freestyle Footbag Club (AFFC)
description:
We are a small Auckland-based club who invite anyone living in
New Zealand or just visiting NZ to come and join us. If you're keen
to play some footbag just leave me some E-Mail and we can sort
something out. :)

Our club helped create a HUGE database of 
freestyle 
moves updated periodically on this website.

name: Austin Style Footbag Club - Texas Footbag League
description: We try to meet a few times a year in Austin, Dallas or Houston.  Check out Texas Footbag League on Facebook.  https://www.facebook.com/groups/216661849514732/

name: Alive And Kickin'
description:
No club - lonesome single player!
Training myself like the time will permit it.
 Or training with a good friend of mine: bunnyleg. Once in two years ;-)

name: Fucheros Unidos del Ateneo
description:
We are one of the largest groups in Caracas, Venezuela, who regularly 
(weekends) get together to play. We invite anyone living in or just visiting 
Caracas to come kick it around. We meet informally year-round (at the place 
and times below), and occasionally hold freestyle and net competitions. In our group, we've got all types of players: beginners who've just kicked 
a footbag for the first time, intermediate and advanced players; players who 
are really into freestyle or footbag net or both (most of us); girls and guys 
of all ages. Come on down! When we get together to play some of us offer for sale hand-made 
Venezuelan 
footbags (which we here call fushi-bolas) at very reasonable prices. These 
are: 2-60 panel leather type, crocheted (sipa-sipa type) and synthetics 
(8,12,14,20,32 and 60 panel soft freestyle type).

name: TEAM NET WRECKER
description:
We are a group of die hard net players.  Currently we have open,
intermediate, and novice players.  We always welcome new
players to kick with us no matter what skill level.  If you 
have never played net before and would like to try it, we
are willing to teach new players about the game.  
Yes we do play footbag golf (proudly infact).  Jamie is the 2000 
and 2001 World Footbag Golf Champ.  It's a lot of fun so don't knock
 it till you've tried it.

name: Sole Power
description:
Hey there,Thanks for stopping by and checking out our club. We are not currently kicking anywhere regularly but if you call us we would be glad to break out our footbags and kick with you. I am in Back China now kicking Chinese feather style hacks, but Tara is still kicking and teaching at local schools. 

PLEASE FEEL FREE TO CALL TARA OR BRIAN TO START A NEW CLUB AT YOUR SCHOOL!

name: Czech Footbag Association
description:
Welcome,

Czech Footbag Association (CFA) is a public non-profit
organization that was founded in January 2002. CFA is
here to organize and develop the footbag sport on the
entire area of CR, to ensure the development and
popularity increase of all footbag sports, to take
care of the national representation and to represent
the Czech footbag in CR and abroad, to set up the
organization rules, technical and economical
restrictions. To supervise the world footbag rules
fulfilment on Czech footbag competitions and to
satisfy all interests of CFA members, increase CFA
members and the methodical management of footbag
clubs.

name: Dallas Footbag Club
description:
Hola!  Call me for freestyle or net.  DFC is pretty disassembled, but JR is
still here.  Don't be skeered.

name: Suomen Footbag Liitto ry. (Finnish Footbag Association)
description:
Finnish Footbag Association (FFA) is an official association which was 
founded in the spring of 1995. FFA is one of the largest footbag clubs 
outside of North America. The meaning of the association is to make 
footbag more recognised and more popular in Finland and in the whole 
Europe. Among other tournaments FFA organizes the Finnish Footbag 
Open 
Championships every year. This tournament and the earlier success in 
the 
international tournaments qualifies the best Finnish players that get to 
be 
members of the TEAM FINLAND, which travels to the European and the 
World 
Footbag Championships to represent Finland.

name: Kitsilano Footbag Association
description:
kfa_logo1.gif The Kitsilano Kickers have been an active Footbag club in Vancouver 
B.C. 
Canada since 1986, and a registered B.C. non-profit society since 1994.  
We 
host 1 tournament and 1 social camp/kick event in B.C annually. The focus is mainly footbag net but there are usually circles in the 
background, and people skooling freestyle moves. The Vancouver Open Footbag Net Tournament, is held 
in 
August each year.   For more 
information 
see the 
upcoming events page at: www.footbag.org/events

name: Memphis Footworks
description:
In 2007, the Memphis Footworks club celebrated 26 years of Footbag 
with our annual tournament in 
memory of 
Chris "KC" Allen. We are planning our April 2008 tournament which will be a doubles net event. Our pics: http://www.footbag.org/gallery/showset/footworks Formed by Lee Guenther, our club celebrates all of our 
current and former players. We play net religously on Saturdays / Sundays with
anywhere 
from 8-12 footbaggers.  Our 
current playing roster:  Mike "Willi" Williams, Chuck 
Simmers, Gary Edge, George Clark, Tommy Carls,  Ken
Hamric, Greg James, Lee Guenther, Eric Marsiglia, Ben Alston, Walt Houston, 
David, Chris and Josh.  Members at heart: RB Benedetti, Rit Ritenour,
Chris "Gator" Routh, Andrew Williams and Lani Rector. In memoriam, Chris "KC" 
Allen, who passed away on the net courts Sept. 9, 2001 and Cathy Guenther, 
long-time kicker and supporter. Our primo locale is Mud Island - a beautiful stretch of turf next to the
rolling Mississippi, and we're almost always there on either Saturday or 
Sunday afternoons (oftentimes  both), rain, shine, sleet or mud.  We mostly
play net and some stylin'.  Drop by sometime and let us show you some of our 
southern hospitality!! Map of our kicking location on Mud Island: View Larger
Map

name: MIDWEST FOOTBAG ASSOCIATION
description:
$html The Midwest Footbag Association focuses on the promotion of the sport of 
footbag. We are an
umbrella organization, providing centralized support for all of the clubs in 
our area. There are no
membership dues, all we ask for is a copy of the club roster plus annual 
updates. Our focus is on promotion. The MFA notifies members of upcoming sanctioned 
events by email only. We assist member clubs (and anyone) in the 
establishment, production
and/or promotion of events, including: Weekly Kicking Sites Monthly Tournaments Club Set-up and Maintenance Kicking at Existing Festivals ...and more!

name: New York Footbag Association
description:
The NYFA has been shredding it up for about 20 years now, and we're 
still going strong.  We welcome all levels of proficiency, including "little 
or none".  We have road trips to tournaments...and even have been having our 
own tournament right here in NYC, the NYFA Freestyle Symposium.

  If you're into "hacky sack", and are ready to push the envelope, join us to 
explore a new frontier in foot-eye coordination!

name: OOPS
description:
UPDATE(Oct21,2014): Looking for Los Angeles freestylers for regular kick sessions. Contact Chris Pinkus to get in touch!

toeshred@gmail.com, or by AIM: toeshred

name: Pihlis Puls
description:
Pihlis Puls was founded on July '98 in a Helsinki suburb called Pihlajamaki 
(Pihlis) by three locals Jesse Ruotsalainen, Olli Savolainen (both net 
players) and a freestyler Antti Lehtovirta.

name: Boriken Footbag Club of Puerto Rico
description:
We are a group of Footbag and Takraw players who gather ones in a wile depending on life. 
If you want more information about our club, send us an e-mail,
write or call. Everyone is invited to visit Puerto Rico --the
Shining Star of the Caribbean.  We have great beaches and sun 
all year long.

name: Chicago Lake Coast Kickers
description:
The Chicago Lake Coast Kickers is a group of highly motivated footbag net 
players in the Chicago area.  We host the Windy City Cup annually in June.  
We are always looking for more players in the area, so please feel free to 
drop us a line and let us know you're interested.

name: Rain City Shred
description:
Hello Footbaggers! Rain City Shred is a club for freestylers of all levels, ages and 
backgroungs 
to meet and play footbag. Please feel free to come to our Saturday kicking 
sessions this spring. We have established times and locations so you can 
come 
and meet kickers from all over Washington state. We encourage you to come 
out 
and hack it up!!! We have hosted 6 official Annual Seattle Juggling and Footbag 
Festivals They have all been a huge success! Footbaggers and jugglers come from all over the country to participate. This event occurs in November and you can find all the necessary info through Footbag.org.
Although we have some of the best players in the world here in Seattle there 
are players of all levels that come to the weekly club meeting.

name: children of the sphere
description:
'Children of the Sphere' is essentially an invitation to contact me, Lotus, because I love playing Footbag and meeting other footbaggers.
I have travelled around Australia for many years as a Footbag street performer and sold good quality multi-panel freestyle bags through my 'Elemental Footbags' brand since 1999.
I welcome any Australian or international players to contact me to arrange a meet and feet any time!

name: Stanford University Footbag Club
description:
This is the first club on the internet, and the second University-sanctioned 
club in the world (after U.C. Berkeley). It was founded in 1992 though 
there has been a standing footbag circle at Stanford since 1987. We play weekly on Tuesdays in White Plaza from 3-5pm. New members are always welcome.  If you're new to footbag, we'll 
teach you, starting with the basics.  Or if you already have some experience, 
please come out and freestyle or challenge us to a game of four-square. View Larger Map

name: RNH Footbag
description:
75 Adds is THE footbag freestyle club in Paris and France. We play every
Monday and Wednesday evenings in gyms provided by the RNH footbag club, and every 
Friday night in a gym shared with all types of jugglers. If you need more information, visit the forum of the French Footbag Association (AFF) website.
Feel free to contact us if you're coming to Paris. It would be a pleasure to meet, shred 
and party with you! Contact us by phone, mail, or on the AFF forum. 
See you in Paris!
